description: Fire or smoke door propped open with a non-magnetic hold-open device such as a wedge, chair, or trash can, defeating smoke compartment integrity
visual_signals: door wedge under fire door
chair holding fire door open
trash can used as door stop
self-closing device bypassed with strap or hook

description: Fire-rated door with gap between door and frame exceeding allowable clearance limits, compromising smoke and fire containment
visual_signals: visible light through fire door edge gap
daylight showing between door and frame
warped fire door not seating against frame
smoke seal missing from door edge

description: Fire-rated door assembly missing its required fire rating label on the door leaf or frame
visual_signals: no metal label on door hinge edge
painted-over label on fire door
missing frame label near top hinge
label location shows removed adhesive residue

description: Corridor clear width reduced below required minimum by parked equipment, beds, carts, or other obstructions
visual_signals: hospital bed parked against corridor wall
equipment lining both sides of hallway
IV poles and carts narrowing passage
wheelchair blocking corridor width

description: Items stored in corridors including boxes, supplies, or linen carts constituting combustible fuel load in egress path
visual_signals: cardboard boxes stacked in hallway
linen cart left in corridor
supply bins stored against corridor wall
paper products outside storage room in hallway

description: Ceiling tiles missing, displaced, broken, or showing water stains indicating breach of smoke-tight barrier above corridor
visual_signals: gap in ceiling grid exposing plenum
brown water stain on ceiling tile
tile sagging or pushed up out of grid
crumbled or broken ceiling tile

description: Exit sign not illuminated, partially lit, or missing from its required location above an egress door or at a corridor junction
visual_signals: dark exit sign above door
exit sign with unlit letters
missing sign above egress doorway
flickering exit sign

description: Emergency lighting unit visibly damaged, battery pack missing, or lamp head broken preventing activation during power failure
visual_signals: cracked emergency light lens
empty emergency light housing on wall
broken lamp head on battery unit
emergency light fixture hanging from wires

description: Fire extinguisher missing from its designated cabinet or bracket, or access to extinguisher obstructed by equipment or storage
visual_signals: empty fire extinguisher bracket
equipment blocking extinguisher cabinet
no extinguisher visible where sign indicates
cart parked in front of extinguisher

description: Fire alarm pull station blocked, obstructed, or not accessible within the required unobstructed path
visual_signals: furniture placed in front of pull station
equipment blocking red pull handle
pull station hidden behind posted notices
cart parked against wall-mounted pull station

description: Items hung from, attached to, or resting on sprinkler piping, placing unauthorized load on the fire suppression system

coat hanger on sprinkler branch line
holiday decorations draped on sprinkler piping
sign wired to sprinkler pipe

description: Sprinkler head obstructed by stored items, shelving, or equipment within the required 18-inch clearance below the deflector
visual_signals: boxes stacked within 18 inches of sprinkler head
shelf positioned directly below sprinkler

items stored high on shelving near ceiling sprinkler

description: Sharps disposal container filled beyond the designated fill line, creating needlestick injury risk during disposal
visual_signals: needles visible above fill line mark
sharps container lid not closing fully
overstuffed red sharps container
sharps protruding from container opening

description: Oxygen cylinders stored without being secured to a wall bracket, chain, rack, or cart, creating a projectile hazard
visual_signals: free-standing oxygen cylinder against wall
unsecured cylinder leaning in corner
cylinder without chain or bracket visible
multiple cylinders grouped without rack

description: Extension cords or relocatable power taps used as substitute for permanent wiring in patient care areas
visual_signals: extension cord running across floor to equipment
power strip plugged into another power strip
multi-outlet adapter on wall receptacle
extension cord taped to wall or baseboard

description: Electrical outlet cover plate missing, cracked, or damaged, exposing energized components in patient-accessible area
visual_signals: missing outlet cover showing wiring
cracked face plate on receptacle
outlet plate hanging by one screw
broken switch plate in patient room

description: Patient bathroom grab bars missing, loose, or pulled away from wall where required for fall prevention
visual_signals: empty screw holes where grab bar was mounted
grab bar wobbling at bracket

description: Biohazard waste container without proper lid closure or missing required biohazard labeling
visual_signals: open-top biohazard waste bin
red bag container missing biohazard symbol
biohazard container lid propped open
unlabeled waste receptacle with red liner

description: Stained, damaged, or non-cleanable ceiling tiles in operating rooms or procedural areas compromising aseptic environment
visual_signals: water stain on OR ceiling tile
crumbling ceiling tile in procedure room

description: Alcohol-based hand rub dispenser installed directly adjacent to or above an electrical outlet or switch, creating ignition risk
visual_signals: hand sanitizer dispenser mounted next to outlet

description: Medications left unattended, unsecured, or accessible in unlocked areas outside the direct supervision of authorized staff

open medication cart in empty hallway
unlocked medication cabinet door ajar

description: Food items or beverages stored directly on the floor rather than on shelves at least six inches above floor level

beverage boxes placed directly on floor

supply containers resting on floor in dietary area

description: Electrical panel clearance violated by stored items, equipment, or furniture within the required 36-inch working space

description: Unsealed penetrations, gaps, or holes in fire-rated wall or floor assemblies from cabling, piping, or construction modifications

description: Emergency vehicle access lane or fire department connection blocked by parked vehicles, dumpsters, or construction materials

description: Narcotics cabinet or controlled substance storage found unlocked, with door ajar, or missing required dual-lock mechanism

description: Expired medications visible on shelving, in carts, or in automated dispensing machines past their use-by date

description: Non-slip flooring absent or floor surface visibly wet without caution signage in patient care or ambulation areas
